task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: Sales/Service Organization IDentifier (SOID)

abstract.p: To enable population management per service organization, we need a method to determine each service organization uniquely. By doing so, the service organization can only see and control its own machines in the service portals.
An identifier (SOID) has been implemented. This identifier is unique for each service organization and can be created and obtained via the service master data portal.

We use the SOID for the direct organization and also for indirect organizations, like our partners. The organization that sells the machines is considered the owner of the service contract. It can decide to do the service by itself or outsource the service to another Service Organization. The SOID is implemented as a hierarchy. The visibility of machines is limited to your own organization and the underlying organizations.

Once the SOID is known, it must be entered into the print system. This can be done for new machines during installation in the installation wizard, or in the settings of the engine for machines already in the field. Having the SOID filled is a prerequisite to properly working with the On Remote Service (ORS) portal; https://prismaservice.cpp.canon.

A check mechanism has been implemented to make sure that only valid entries are made. A verification code must be entered that matches the SOID. This verification code is calculated from the entered SOID. No online connection to a remote server is needed. Only correct matches between SOID and verification code are accepted.

section.title: Service Masterdata Portal
section.p: The Service MasterData Portal is available for authorized users via http://servicemd.cpp.canon
Under the tab 'Service Organization' you find the setup of all service organizations.
The SOID is implemented as a hierarchy. The visibility of machines is limited to your own organization and the underlying organizations.
When you select a specific service organization, the details for that organization are shown. This includes the SOID and the verification code.

section.title: How to become authorized
section.p: Authorization is controlled top down. HQ authorizes RSHQ, RSHQ authorizes NSO and regional dealers, and NSO authorizes local dealers.

section.title: Usage of the SOID
section.p: There are several configuration aspects. An organization that (partly) outsources the service activities must configure who its outsourcing parties are. These are called Servicing Partners. Per machine can be indicated with which servicing partner the service is shared.

section.title: Configuration at the organization level
section.p: An organization that outsources machine service, needs to configure its servicing partners.

By default, the list of service organizations will show all the organizations that you are allowed to see. Configuring additional servicing partners is done in the ORS portal (https://PRISMAservice.cpp.canon). Multiple Servicing partners can be configured.
Login on the ORS portal,

li.p: Select the '+' sign to add a servicing partner.
A popup is shown where you need to fill out the SOID code and the matching verification code.

section.p: The SOID of the Canon organizations can be found in the TSM.
See: What to do when you do not know the SOID.

section.title: Configuration at engine level
section.p: When installing an engine, during the installation wizard is requested to enter the SOID.
The SOID of the organization that sold the machine needs to be filled out.
See: 11 How to change the SOID (Sales/Service Organization Identifier) of the system.

Delegating service of a machine is done per machine, as every machine can have a different servicing organization e.g. due to geographical reasons. Delegation can only be done by the owner of the service contract. In practice, this means that the SOID of the machine must be equal to that of the person logged in to the service portal.
A machine that has its service being outsourced, needs to be shared explicitly with the organization it is outsourced to.
By doing so, the machine can also be taken along in the service reporting of the outsourced organization.

Depending on the hierarchy it can be that the machine is already visible. Report filtering requires the sharing of an individual machine. Sharing an individual machine is done in ORS (https://prismaservice.cpp.canon ).

li.p: Login on the PRISMAservice portal, search and select the machine.
Select the edit button of Service delegated to
Select the pull down of the Service delegated to and select one of your earlier configured preferred servicing partners. This list will show all the organizations in your scope by default.

p.b: Correcting the SOID ( only for temporary correction)

section.p: When the SOID was configured incorrectly it needs to be corrected at the machine. It is possible to override the SOID of the machine in the ORS settings of the machine.
This must be temporary and changed at next visit at the machine. Rationale is that information retrieved locally at the engine the SOID is still set incorrectly.

li.p: Login on the PRISMAservice portal, search the machine in the Product selector and open it.
Select the edit icon right beside the Service organization
Select the pull down below the New and select one of your earlier configured servicing partners.
Next step: change the SOID on the machine. See: 11 How to change the SOID (Sales/Service Organization Identifier) of the system.

section.title: What to do when SOID is not known
section.p: In the installation wizard it is mandatory to fill in the SOID. If the correct SOID is not known, it is advised to enter the RSHQ or HQ SOID.
See: What to do when you do not know the SOID.
